matches.title: Data Scientist
matches.reasoning: Your solid foundation in Python and SQL, combined with your preference for problem-solving and building quantitative models, makes Data Science a perfect fit. Your background in statistics and analytical thinking aligns directly with industry needs.

tasks: Master Pandas and NumPy for complex data manipulation.
Complete a dataset cleaning project using Jupyter Notebooks.
Learn Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) techniques.
Study probability distributions, hypothesis testing, and regression analysis.
Learn Tableau or PowerBI to build interactive business dashboards.
Create a visualization project showing insights from a public dataset (e.g., Kaggle).
Learn supervised learning algorithms (Linear Regression, Decision Trees, SVM).
Understand model evaluation metrics (Precision, Recall, ROC-AUC).
Build a predictive model using Scikit-Learn.
Study clustering algorithms (K-Means, Hierarchical Clustering).
Learn dimensionality reduction using PCA.
Perform feature engineering on raw data to improve model score.
Master window functions, CTEs, and query optimization in SQL.
Get familiar with Spark or basic cloud data storage (AWS S3 / Google BigQuery).
Build an end-to-end ETL pipeline script.
Develop 3 polished GitHub repositories showcasing data projects.
Write a blog post explaining one of your machine learning models.
Practice SQL and ML coding challenges on LeetCode/HackerRank.

matches.title: Machine Learning Engineer
matches.reasoning: You enjoy building concrete engineering systems and have strong Python skills. ML engineering focuses more on production deployment, model serving, and pipeline automation, which matches your 'building' work preference.

tasks: Learn Neural Network fundamentals (Forward/Backward propagation).
Build basic models using PyTorch or TensorFlow.
Train a simple image classifier or text classifier.
Learn OOP principles in Python and write modular, clean code.
Master advanced Git commands, branching, and pull requests.
Write unit tests for your data preprocessing functions.
Build a REST API using FastAPI or Flask to serve model predictions.
Learn Docker and containerize your ML api service.
Deploy your container locally and test with Postman.
Deploy your Docker container to AWS ECS or GCP Cloud Run.
Understand CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions) for automatic builds.
Set up basic logging and model tracking (MLflow).
Learn Airflow for orchestrating data pipelines.
Understand feature stores and data versioning (DVC).
Build an automated retrain pipeline trigger.
Build an end-to-end production ML system (Scraping -> Training -> API -> Cloud).
Read system design blogs from Netflix, Uber, and Airbnb.
Prepare for ML system design interview questions.

matches.title: Data Analyst
matches.reasoning: A highly practical starting path. You already have Python and SQL skills, which are 70% of a Data Analyst's toolkit. This role allows you to break into the industry quickly before transitioning to complex ML roles.

tasks: Learn advanced SQL joins, subqueries, and window functions.
Start learning Tableau or Power BI.
Recreate a standard business dashboard using a sample dataset.
Understand core business KPIs (CAC, LTV, Churn, ARR).
Learn how to translate business problems into data queries.
Write a business brief detailing insights from a sales dataset.
Learn statistical significance and hypothesis testing.
Understand A/B testing frameworks and sizing.
Analyze a mock A/B test run and present recommendations.
Learn dashboard design principles (color theory, layout hierarchy).
Build a highly polished, interactive executive dashboard.
Practice presenting findings to a non-technical audience.
